title: The Subatomic Registry

features.name: the infinitesimal index
features.desc-on-touch: filing cabinets so small they could fit in your palm, each drawer labeled with particles or moments; you pull one and find not papers but the very concept of papers, crystallized

features.name: quantum ledger wall
features.desc-on-touch: names written so small they're barely visible, each one a memory too fragile to survive being fully seen; the wall hums at frequencies that make your teeth ache

features.name: the unnameable cell
features.desc-on-touch: one room exists here so small only your awareness can enter; you feel the weight of being unnamed, and understand that this privacy is a mercy the archive grants to certain things

features.name: photon filing system
features.desc-on-touch: folders of pure light arranged on shelves of compressed shadow; the files are accessible only by looking at them indirectly—direct observation causes them to scatter and reshelve themselves

features.name: the dust registry
features.desc-on-touch: a catalogue of things so small they are barely-there; cataloguing requires accepting that the thing being described will vanish in the act of description, and this archive has made peace with that loss

inscription: here, smaller than naming, the archive discovered that intimacy is measured in inverse proportion to publicity; some truths live only at the scale where they cannot be spoken

file.name: Particle_Decay_Moment_Lattice_Microdata.tmp
file.note: a spectrometry reading with only statistical noise, until the corruption rearranged the noise pattern into handwriting: 'I knew you would come looking for this. —K'

reverie.title: REVERIE — the smallest place where a thing is still itself
narr: you kneel together in a room the size of heartbeats; she
holds something smaller than words. the light here is soft,
almost too small to be visible. she says: "this is where
private things live. not because they're hidden, but because
this is the only scale at which they remain completely true."

exits.label: where small things gather into community
toward scales that have no name yet
